title: Stash Internals, Partial Work, and Portable Patches · Conflict Triage
summary: A responder inherits a plausible but defective work-in-progress preservation and transfer ledger; preserve reachable history, isolate the first causal Git-state mismatch, and retain a regression probe. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in work-in-progress preservation and transfer ledger and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Inspect stash commit structure, parents, refs, reflog, staged state, and untracked representation instead of treating a stash as an external bag.
Use pathspec, patch, staged, keep-index, include-untracked, and all controls while previewing secret and ignored-file consequences.
Choose apply, pop, branch, store, create, export, or import from collision risk, portability, and retention needs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record
steps.instruction:
debug case — Case G87 is a debug in a disposable repository. only reviewed staged work should move while unstaged experiments remain visible. The learner must predict the stash records staged state and the working tree keeps unrelated unstaged paths, run git stash push --staged --message urgent-policy-wip, explain policy changes moved to stash@{0} while the experiment remains unstaged for inspection, test this changed condition: add an untracked credential fixture and reject include-untracked until secret review passes, and preserve this recovery boundary: create a named branch or bundle for valuable work before expiring the temporary stash. Competency probe: Inspect stash commit structure, parents, refs, reflog, staged state, and untracked representation instead of treating a stash as an external bag.

Inspect the Gita Stash Object Model trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for inspect stash commit structure, parents, refs, reflog, staged state, and untracked representation instead of treating a stash as an external bag.

content.text: For Gita Stash Object Model, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: Name the Gita Stash Object Model precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Case G87 is a debug in a disposable repository. only reviewed staged work should move while unstaged experiments remain visible. The learner must predict the stash records staged state and the working tree keeps unrelated unstaged paths, run git stash push --staged --message urgent-policy-wip, explain policy changes moved to stash@{0} while the experiment remains unstaged for inspection, test this changed condition: add an untracked credential fixture and reject include-untracked until secret review passes, and preserve this recovery boundary: create a named branch or bundle for valuable work before expiring the temporary stash. Competency probe: Inspect stash commit structure, parents, refs, reflog, staged state, and untracked representation instead of treating a stash as an external bag.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Stashed changes live outside the object database and cannot affect retention.

stimulus.title: Gita Stash Object Model case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Case G87 is a debug in a disposable repository. only reviewed staged work should move while unstaged experiments remain visible. The learner must predict the stash records staged state and the working tree keeps unrelated unstaged paths, run git stash push --staged --message urgent-policy-wip, explain policy changes moved to stash@{0} while the experiment remains unstaged for inspection, test this changed condition: add an untracked credential fixture and reject include-untracked until secret review passes, and preserve this recovery boundary: create a named branch or bundle for valuable work before expiring the temporary stash. Competency probe: Inspect stash commit structure, parents, refs, reflog, staged state, and untracked representation instead of treating a stash as an external bag.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Stashed changes live outside the object database and cannot affect retention.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Inspect stash commit structure, parents, refs, reflog, staged state, and untracked representation instead of treating a stash as an external bag. Artifact under review: work-in-progress preservation and transfer ledger.

options.text: For inspect the failure record, apply Gita Stash Object Model without its stated preconditions, omit the work-in-progress preservation and transfer ledger trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Gita Stash Object Model for work-in-progress preservation and transfer ledger.
The record names the assumption, applies Gita Stash Object Model, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Gita Stash Object Model record says work-in-progress preservation and transfer ledger looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. This does not resolve inspect the failure record.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-wip-recovery-policy-5tomnf" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Wip Recovery Policy into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-wip-recovery-policy-5tomnf
# scenario-key: trace-5tomnf probe-fnmot5 boundary-omnf5t owner-5tomnfx fault-5tomnfz
# before-ref: main=a11ce5t candidate=b00b1nf
# hypothesis: only reviewed staged work should move while unstaged experiments remain visible
git stash push --staged --message urgent-policy-wip
# expected: the stash records staged state and the working tree keeps unrelated unstaged paths
# observation: policy changes moved to stash@{0} while the experiment remains unstaged for inspection
# changed-case: add an untracked credential fixture and reject include-untracked until secret review passes
# after-ref: main=a11ce5t candidate=c0ffeef
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: create a named branch or bundle for valuable work before expiring the temporary stash
# transfer-limit: inspect real ignored and untracked paths because stash can store sensitive bytes in Git objects
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Gita Wip Recovery Policy structural check is only one gate. work-in-progress preservation and transfer ledger must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

hints: Name the Gita Wip Recovery Policy precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Case G88 is a debug in a disposable repository. only reviewed staged work should move while unstaged experiments remain visible. The learner must predict the stash records staged state and the working tree keeps unrelated unstaged paths, run git stash push --staged --message urgent-policy-wip, explain policy changes moved to stash@{0} while the experiment remains unstaged for inspection, test this changed condition: add an untracked credential fixture and reject include-untracked until secret review passes, and preserve this recovery boundary: create a named branch or bundle for valuable work before expiring the temporary stash. Competency probe: Create named durable refs or encrypted external backups for important work, verify restoration on a changed base, and expire temporary objects deliberately.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: A stash is an appropriate durable backup and collaboration channel.

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write the debug evidence note for Gita Wip Recovery Policy: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Case G95 is a debug in a disposable repository. only reviewed staged work should move while unstaged experiments remain visible. The learner must predict the stash records staged state and the working tree keeps unrelated unstaged paths, run git stash push --staged --message urgent-policy-wip, explain policy changes moved to stash@{0} while the experiment remains unstaged for inspection, test this changed condition: add an untracked credential fixture and reject include-untracked until secret review passes, and preserve this recovery boundary: create a named branch or bundle for valuable work before expiring the temporary stash. steps.why: Retrieving Gita Wip Recovery Policy through the concrete work-in-progress preservation and transfer ledger case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Create named durable refs or encrypted external backups for important work, verify restoration on a changed base, and expire temporary objects deliberately.
